# ChatOn

> ChatOn is a free, decentralized, end-to-end encrypted messaging app built on the
> DeSo blockchain. Available as a progressive web app at getchaton.com. No phone
> number required. Near-zero infrastructure cost. Censorship-resistant message delivery.

## About
- [What is ChatOn](https://getchaton.com/about): Overview of ChatOn, how it works, features, and tradeoffs
- [FAQ](https://getchaton.com/faq): Frequently asked questions about ChatOn, encryption, and DeSo
- [Compare](https://getchaton.com/compare): Honest feature comparison of ChatOn vs Signal, Telegram, WhatsApp, and Session
- [Community Directory](https://getchaton.com/community): Browse and join public ChatOn groups
- [Privacy Policy](https://getchaton.com/privacy): ChatOn privacy policy
- [Terms of Service](https://getchaton.com/terms): ChatOn terms of service
- [Support](https://getchaton.com/support): Support ChatOn development

## Source Code
- [GitHub](https://github.com/sungkhum/chaton): ChatOn open source repository

## Key Facts
- End-to-end encrypted by default using ECDH key exchange and AES-128-CTR (all messages, DMs and groups)
- No phone number or email required to sign up
- Messages stored as encrypted transactions on the DeSo blockchain
- Progressive Web App (no app store required, works on any device)
- Near-zero infrastructure cost (DeSo blockchain is the data layer)
- Open source (GitHub: github.com/sungkhum/chaton)
- Each message costs roughly $0.000017 in DESO transaction fees
- Features: encrypted group chats, emoji reactions, GIFs and stickers, image/video sharing, file attachments, reply threads, push notifications, community directory, unread badges
- ChatOn (getchaton.com) is a decentralized messaging app. It is not related to chaton.ai or Samsung's discontinued ChatON service.
